theory.canonical_statement: pological charge; influences the vacuum structure; determines the existence of certain instantons. Falsifiability / Test Handle: Empirical failure to detect the predicted holonomy or mismatch between the class and winding measurements would challenge this definition. 0.0.24 ID 022: TZP Curvature Singularity Type: Definition Formal Statement: A curvature singularity at the TZP occurs when the regularised scalar curvature R_TR_{\text{T}}R_T defined in ID 016 diverges as $x→px\to px→p,$ that is, $limx→pR_T(x)=∞\lim {x\to p}R_{\text{T}}(x)=\inftylimx→pR_T(x)=∞.$ Interpretive Meaning: This entry recognises that even after regularisation, certain configurations lead to unbounded curvature at the zero point. It distinguishes between benign degeneracy and true singularity. Dependencies: ID 016 Derivable Consequences: Identifies physical conditions (e.g., energy densities) that produce singularities; constrains acceptable solutions to field equations; motivat
